name: Syn
description: Syn is a Norse goddess associated with doorways, boundaries, and justice. Her name translates to "refusal." As a member of the Aesir, Syn guards the doors of Frigg's hall, Fensalir, and is responsible for denying entry to those deemed unworthy. She is invoked in matters concerning legal disputes and boundaries, acting as a protector of justice and fairness. Syn's power lies in her ability to discern truth and falsehood, ensuring that only the righteous are granted access and that transgressors are kept at bay. While her role is often subtle, it is crucial in maintaining order and upholding the laws of the gods and humans alike. She is a steadfast guardian, unwavering in her duty to protect the sanctity of boundaries and the pursuit of justice. She is not widely mentioned in surviving texts, but her name and function strongly suggest a significant role in Norse social and legal structures. Some scholars believe she may have been more prominent in earlier periods of Norse religion. Her associations with doors also link her to the passage between realms and the protection of sacred spaces. She is a figure of strength and unwavering resolve, embodying the concept of refusal in the face of injustice and unworthiness. Syn's domain extends to all matters requiring discernment and the enforcement of ethical boundaries.
subtitle: Goddess of Refusal and Doorways

keyMyths.title: Syn Guards Fensalir
keyMyths.description: Syn is the guardian of the doors to Frigg's hall, Fensalir, denying entry to anyone deemed unworthy or unjust. Her role highlights her dedication to upholding standards of righteousness.

keyMyths.title: Invocation in Legal Matters
keyMyths.description: While no specific myth details this, it is believed Syn was invoked in legal disputes to ensure a fair judgment. Her association with justice made her a powerful figure in resolving conflicts.

keyMyths.title: Protection of Boundaries
keyMyths.description: Syn's role as a guardian extends beyond physical doors to the protection of all boundaries, both physical and metaphorical. She ensures that limits are respected and that order is maintained.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A primary source for Norse mythology, mentioning Syn among the Aesir goddesses.
sources.text: A primary source for Norse mythology, mentioning Syn among the Aesir goddesses.

sources.author: Rudolf Simek
